rulings.Case Title: MONA MILFORD ET AL VS. D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-604855, CGC-23-608547, CGC-23-607017 WITH CASE CGC-22-603644
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 21, 2024, Line 2. DEFENDANT D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC AND ANDREW NICHOLLS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-604855, CGC-23-608547, CGC-23-607017 WITH CASE CGC-22-603644. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on September 6, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all cases arising from tenant/landlord disputes per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Both parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than August 26, 2024. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN REINER VS. TAYUAN MA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Require Security Under Vexatious Litigant Statutes; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Rita Himes In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 21, 2024, Line 3. DEFENDANT THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Dismiss Or Require Security Under Vexatious Litigant Statutes. Defendant the State Bar of California's motion to dismiss is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RIANN TAVU VS. SBM MANAFEMENT SERVICES, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 21, 2024, Line 9. DEFENDANT SBM FACILITY SERVICES, LLC, CONWY, LLC, CHARLES SOMERS LIVING TRUST AND SBM MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LP's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendants SBM Management Services, LP, SBM Facility Services, LLC, Conwy, LLC, and Charles Somers Living Trust's motion to compel arbitration is granted in part and denied in part.
